intro_narration.text: Aldera, in the shadow of Mount Aurin. Three years since the storm took the east bank — and Caled with it.
The mountain has been glowing at night again. Nobody says so out loud.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: Three stones gone, and my village still standing under a waking mountain. I do not know which of those facts to trust.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: Whoever they were, they knew the wards better than Edrin does. That frightens me more than the theft.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: Rest tonight. Tomorrow we speak of who follows them.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: The shrine path is east, past the old pond. Edrin will talk your ear off — let him. He notices what others miss.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: Aren. Good — I was about to send for you.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: Maester Edrin means to climb to the Aurin Shrine and read the wards. After last night's lights, I will not let him go alone.

lines.speaker: Elder Thane
lines.text: Take Bram, if you can pry him from the forge. The east path, past the pond. Go carefully.

lines.speaker: Bram
lines.text: Someone broke a seal that held for centuries, and we were a day late. A day!

lines.speaker: Bram
lines.text: I'm coming with you. Don't even start.

lines.speaker: Bram
lines.text: The elder's sending you up the mountain? And I hear it from you, not from him?

lines.speaker: Bram
lines.text: Fine. I'll get my gear. You're not doing this without me.

lines.speaker: Liora
lines.text: Tracks, Edrin said. Five sets. One of them dragging the left foot. Aren — Caled walked like that, after the rockfall year.

lines.speaker: Liora
lines.text: Don't look at me like that. I'm not grieving him twice. If he's alive, I'm going with you.

lines.speaker: Liora
lines.text: Mother lit a candle for Caled again last night. Three years, and she still sets his place at the table.

lines.speaker: Liora
lines.text: I've stopped telling her to stop.

lines.speaker: Maester Edrin
lines.text: Four sockets. Three empty. The earth stone untouched — passed over, or left on purpose? The difference matters enormously.

lines.speaker: Maester Edrin
lines.text: I catalogued those wards for thirty years and never once asked who else might be reading my notes. That question will keep me up tonight.

lines.speaker: Maester Edrin
lines.text: The lights above the peak follow a pattern, you know. Brighter on the ninth night. Nobody else counts.

lines.speaker: Maester Edrin
lines.text: When the elder gives the word, we climb. I have waited half my life to stand inside that shrine.
